clean_code:
# Option 1: exclude assets with missing features
missing = np.any(np.isnan(X), axis=1)
out[missing] = np.nan  # signal these as invalid
X = X[~missing]

# Option 2: impute with column mean, not zero
col_means = np.nanmean(X, axis=0)
nan_mask = np.isnan(X)
X[nan_mask] = np.take(col_means, np.where(nan_mask)[1])
description: Missing feature values are replaced with zero before model inference.
explanation: Zero is rarely a neutral value for financial features. For a momentum feature, zero means 'no momentum.' For a volatility feature, zero means 'no volatility.' Replacing NaN with zero injects artificial signals that the model was never trained on, causing unpredictable predictions for assets with missing data.
suggested_fix: Either exclude assets with missing features from predictions (set out[:] = np.nan for those assets) or impute with the column mean/median computed on non-missing data.

issues.title: Zero imputation for missing features introduces artificial signals
Zero imputation for missing features introduces artificial signals
